question: [File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who controls the mirror REST endpoint (via MITM on an HTTP mirrorRest URL) inject a node list containing only a targeted subset of real consensus nodes so that submitWithRetry() routes all transfer transactions exclusively to those nodes, causing ≥30% of network processing nodes to be overwhelmed and shut down without brute force?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external attacker intercept the unauthenticated HTTP GET to cfg.mirrorRest and return a crafted nodesEnvelope containing only endpoints for a targeted 30%+ subset of real network nodes, causing fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() to build a network map that concentrates all pinger traffic on those nodes until they shut down?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker exploit the fact that buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() never follows the Links.Next pagination field, so by controlling the first page of the mirror REST /network/nodes response they can omit 70%+ of nodes and concentrate all SDK traffic on the remaining targeted nodes, causing those nodes to shut down under load?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: backoff()] Can an unprivileged external attacker set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES to a value ≥63 so that the expression 1<<(attempt-1) in backoff() overflows a 64-bit integer, producing a zero or negative duration, causing submitWithRetry() to fire transfer transactions at maximum speed against targeted nodes and shut down ≥30% of them?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external attacker set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL to a near-zero duration (e.g., 1ns) so that the ticker in main() fires submitWithRetry() at an extremely high rate, flooding targeted consensus nodes with transfer transactions and causing ≥30% of them to shut down?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external attacker serve a crafted nodesEnvelope JSON response with no io.LimitReader protection on resp.Body, causing json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode() to consume unbounded memory and crash the pinger process, which removes it as a health-monitoring signal and masks the shutdown of ≥30% of network nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker who controls DNS for the mirrorRest hostname perform DNS rebinding to redirect the HTTP client to an internal address, causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to fetch a poisoned node list that routes all pinger transactions to targeted nodes, contributing to their shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external attacker set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES to a large value (e.g., 100) so that submitWithRetry() retries up to 101 times per tick against the same targeted node (since the SDK may pin to a node on failure), causing that node to receive a sustained burst of transactions and shut down?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external attacker inject serviceEndpoint entries with duplicate host:port addresses for the same targeted node account IDs, causing the network map built by fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() to map multiple logical node identities to the same physical node, concentrating all SDK traffic on that node and shutting it down?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_NETWORK=other and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_REST to an attacker-controlled HTTP URL, causing buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() to fetch a poisoned node list with no TLS or authentication, and route all pinger transactions to targeted nodes to cause their shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external attacker set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_BASE_BACKOFF to 0 or a negative duration so that backoff() returns 0 for every retry attempt, causing submitWithRetry() to hammer targeted nodes with rapid-fire transactions and shut down ≥30% of them?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external attacker return a nodesEnvelope with thousands of nodeEntry records all pointing to the same targeted node's IP, causing the network map to be dominated by that node's address and all pinger transactions to be routed there, shutting it down?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external attacker set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_AMOUNT_TINYBAR to math.MinInt64 so that hiero.HbarFromTinybar(-cfg.amountTinybar) causes an integer overflow, producing a malformed transfer transaction that is rejected by all nodes except targeted ones, causing those nodes to process invalid state and shut down?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external attacker serve a response where serviceEndpoint.Port is set to 0 for all legitimate nodes but non-zero for targeted nodes, causing fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() to skip all legitimate nodes (due to the ep.Port == 0 check) and build a network map containing only targeted nodes, routing all traffic there?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_TIMEOUT to a very large value so that the http.Client hangs indefinitely on a slow-response mirror REST server, blocking the pinger from detecting node failures and masking the shutdown of ≥30% of network nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external attacker serve a nodesEnvelope where all nodeEntry.NodeAccountID fields are empty strings, causing fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() to skip all nodes and return an error, preventing the pinger from submitting health-check transactions and masking the shutdown of ≥30% of network nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external attacker set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_TO_ACCOUNT_ID to a non-existent or frozen account so that every transfer in submitWithRetry() fails with a receipt error, causing the retry loop to exhaust all attempts and hammer targeted nodes with failing transactions, contributing to their shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: backoff()] Can an unprivileged external attacker set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_BASE_BACKOFF to a very large duration (e.g., 24h) so that backoff() returns an extremely long sleep, causing submitWithRetry() to stall indefinitely and prevent the pinger from detecting and reporting the shutdown of ≥30% of network nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external attacker serve a nodesEnvelope with serviceEndpoint entries where DomainName contains a crafted hostname that resolves to the IP of a targeted consensus node, causing net.JoinHostPort() to build an address pointing to that node and concentrating all pinger traffic there to cause its shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker exploit the absence of HTTPS enforcement on cfg.mirrorRest to perform a man-in-the-middle attack, replacing the legitimate node list with one containing only targeted nodes, causing the Hiero SDK client to route all transactions to those nodes and shut them down?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external attacker set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_INTERVAL to 1ns and HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MAX_RETRIES to 62 so that the combined effect of near-zero interval and near-zero backoff (due to overflow) causes submitWithRetry() to submit thousands of transactions per second to targeted nodes, shutting down ≥30% of them?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external attacker serve a response with a Content-Type that causes json.NewDecoder to misparse the body, returning a partially populated nodesEnvelope that includes only targeted nodes in the network map, routing all pinger traffic to those nodes?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes()] Can an unprivileged external attacker set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_MIRROR_NODE_CLIENT_MAX_RETRIES to a very large value so that buildNetworkFromMirrorNodes() retries fetching a poisoned node list many times, and once it succeeds with the poisoned list, the SDK routes all traffic to targeted nodes causing their shutdown?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: fetchMirrorNodeNetwork()] Can an unprivileged external attacker serve a nodesEnvelope where serviceEndpoint.IPAddressV4 contains a valid IP of a targeted consensus node but serviceEndpoint.DomainName is empty, causing the fallback to IPAddressV4 in fetchMirrorNodeNetwork() to build a network map pointing exclusively to that node?

[File: 'File Name: pinger/transfer.go -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: submitWithRetry()] Can an unprivileged external attacker set HIERO_MIRROR_PINGER_OPERATOR_KEY to the well-known default key (302e020100300506032b65700422042091132178e72057a1d7528025956fe39b0b847f200ab59b2fdd367017f3087137) to impersonate the operator and submit transactions that are routed to targeted nodes, causing those nodes to process fraudulent transfers and shut down?
